narrative.summary:
𝗣𝗘𝗥𝗦𝗢𝗡𝗔𝗟 𝗘𝗩𝗘𝗡𝗧

Dessa's student exhibition at The Cooper Union, East Village.

A chance to see Dessa's work in the context of one of America's most storied art and architecture schools.

blocks.title: Arrival
blocks.body:
𝗔𝗥𝗥𝗜𝗩𝗔𝗟

Cooper Square at E 7th St — the iconic Foundation Building (1859) and the angular Morphosis-designed 41 Cooper Square (2009) anchor the campus.

This is personal. Dessa's student exhibition at Cooper Union — one of the few remaining tuition-free art schools in America, housed in a building that's hosted everyone from Abraham Lincoln to Mark Twain. The East Village location puts you in the heart of downtown Manhattan's creative pulse.

blocks.title: About
blocks.body:
𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧

The Cooper Union for the Advancement of Science and Art has been a pillar of New York's creative community since 1859. Student exhibitions here carry the weight of a school that counts Milton Glaser, Alex Katz, and Tom Otterness among its alumni. The gallery spaces rotate between the Foundation Building and the Morphosis building.

📍 30 Cooper Square, New York, NY 10003
🌐 cooper.edu
📞 (212) 353-4100
